Going back to this program of relutrigine targeting SCN2A and SCN8A DEE patients, this was made possible thanks to positive results from the phase 2/3 EMBOLD study. The beauty of this program is that highly positive interim results warranted the Data Monitoring Committee [DMC] to stop the trial early due to efficacy. At that time, Praxis also mentioned that it was to meet with the FDA to confirm a path forward for the use of relutrigine to treat these specific DEE patients. The double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized study enrolled a total of 16 patients with early-onset SCN2A and SCN8A DEE. These participants were randomized 1:1 to receive either of the following dosing schedules:

  • Relutrigine once daily [QD] for 16 weeks
  • Relutrigine QD for 12 weeks and then matching placebo for 4 weeks.

The main result to pay attention to is the randomized, double-blind portion of the study where relutrigine-treated patients were able to achieve a placebo-adjusted 46% reduction in motor seizure frequency. Not only that, but what's impressive is that about 33% of these patients ended up being seizure-free after the 16-week period as well. There were no drug-related serious adverse events [SAEs] to mention, and the adverse events [AEs] that happened were mild to moderate.

These positive results from the interim analysis of the EMBOLD study, again stopped early due to efficacy, are what allowed the company to submit an NDA of relutrigine for the treatment of these SCN2A/SNC8A DEE patients to the FDA. This U.S. agency not only accepted the NDA of this drug for the treatment of these specific DEE patients but also set a priority review PDUFA target action date of September 27, 2026. What makes this good for the patients is that, should this drug be approved, it would be the first one for them. That's because there are no currently approved therapies to treat these SCN2A and SCN8A DEE patients. It would be the first disease-modifying therapy for them.

The bullish nature of this company, as I highlighted in this article, is the nature of it being able to target the hyperexcitability state of sodium channels, giving it broad potential. This means that it can go beyond that of only targeting certain gene mutations that cause the DEE. With that being said, it is in the process of exploring the use of relutrigine for broad DEEs in the ongoing EMERALD study. If this study ends up being successful, plus barring that relutrigine is approved for U.S. marketing of targeting patients with SCN2A and SCN8A DEE, then it could file a supplemental new drug application [sNDA] for this drug to expand its label. Full enrollment of this EMERALD study is expected to happen in the 2nd half of 2026, with data to be released from it thereafter. Thus, the sNDA might potentially be submitted in 2027.

The second expansion opportunity is actually using an entirely different drug from its pipeline, which is antisense oligonucleotide [ASO] elsunersen, being developed for the treatment of patients with early seizure onset SCN2A DEE. There are two things to mention about this program. The first of which is that the company just released a set of results from Part A of the phase 1/2 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled EMBRAVE study. It stated that elsunersen was able to achieve a 77% placebo-adjusted seizure reduction from baseline. This is pretty good data, and why this program is coming along well, just like relutrigine.

The thing is that the company had another positive development for this program that came its way. After meeting with the FDA in December of 2025, it was noted that the company could remove the sham control arm as part of the other ongoing EMBRAVE3 study for this program. Barring that this study remains on track, the company believes that it is going to be able to release data from it in 2027.

Financials

According to the 10-K SEC Filing, Praxis Precision Medicines had cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities of $926.1 million as of December 31, 2025. The truth is that this company has plenty of cash on hand, especially since it was able to raise additional cash in January of 2026. This is where it enacted a public offering to raise an estimated $575 million. It even granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 331,800 shares as well. The total gross proceeds raised from this offering were $621.2 million.

With the cash of $926.1 million as of December 31, 2025, plus the amount raised through the January 2026 offering, it believes that it will be able to fund its operations into 2028.

The company burns approximately $97 million of cash per quarter. This is broken down into $77.5 million in R&D expenses and then $19.5 million in G&A expenses.

Risks To Business

There are several risks that investors should be aware of before investing in Praxis Precision Medicines. The first risk to consider would be in terms of the submitted NDA of ulixacaltamide for the treatment of patients with ET. For the time being, this regulatory application was submitted to the FDA after a positive pre-NDA meeting with it in December of 2025. The risk here is that there is no assurance that this regulatory application is going to be accepted by the FDA in its current form, nor that the company will receive U.S. marketing approval of ulixacaltamide for the treatment of ET patients.

The second risk to consider would be in terms of the development of relutrigine for the treatment of patients with SCN2A and SNC8A DE. The company was able to stop its trial early due to efficacy and submit its NDA of this drug to the FDA. A priority review PDUFA target action date of September 27, 2026, was set by the agency to review the drug by.

The risk here is that there is no assurance that the FDA is going to approve relutrigine for the treatment of these patients with SCN2A/SCN8A DEE for starters. Another risk to consider here would be the first expansion opportunity I mentioned above, which is the development of this drug to target broad DEE [pan-DEE] patients. This is happening with the ongoing EMERALD study that is in place. The risk here is that the company mentions that should this study have a successful outcome, then it would serve as the basis for it to be able to file an sNDA of relutrigine to target a broad group of DEE patients. The risk here is that there is no guarantee that the final results are going to be positive, despite the fact that the company mentioned that its drug is capable of targeting multiple etiologies of this disease.

The third and final risk to consider would be in terms of the development of ASO elsunersen for the treatment of early seizure onset SCN2A gain of function [GoF] DEE patients being advanced in two studies. The company is currently exploring the use of this drug candidate in the EMBRAVE3 registrational trial treating these specific DEE patients. It already was able to show that it was capable of achieving a 77% placebo-adjusted seizure reduction from baseline from Part A of the Phase 1/2 EMBRAVE study.

The risk here is that there is no assurance that the company will achieve a positive final outcome from the EMBRAVE3 registrational study, nor that the efficacy of what was achieved beforehand is going to be closely similar.

Conclusion

The final conclusion is that Praxis Precision Medicines continues to deliver promising results from its pipeline with a variety of drugs. With respect to ulixacaltamide for the treatment of patients with ET, it was able to submit an NDA to the FDA. In terms of the program of advancing the use of relutrigine for the treatment of patients with SCN2A and SCN8A DEEs, it not only had its NDA accepted by the FDA but was granted a priority review target action PDUFA date of September 27, 2026.

Hopefully, this goes well, because it is already in the process of evaluating the use of this drug for a broad set of DEE patients in DEE in the EMERALD study. What's the point of this? As I mentioned above, being able to go after this broad set of DEE patients will allow it to expand its market opportunity to be >$5 billion. Another item to mention is that relutrigine for the targeting of SCN2A/SCN8A DEE has been granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation [BTD], Rare Pediatric Disease Designation, and Orphan Drug Designation [ODD].

In essence, it has the chance to not only be the first approved therapy for these specific DEE patients, but it could also allow Praxis Precision Medicines to obtain a Pediatric Review Voucher [PRV]. It can either choose to keep this to speed up the review of another drug or end up selling it to generate cash. With everything I mentioned above, I believe that this stock should remain as a Strong Buy rating.
